memberdef.argsstring: (String text, Rectangle rect, const Alignment alignment=Alignment::TopLeft) noexcept
memberdef.name: Text

briefdescription.para: Create a text description for the given content and target rectangle.

parameterdescription.para: The text alignment inside the rectangle.

briefdescription.para: Get the optional text color sequence.

parameterdescription.para: The sequence of colors to apply while rendering the text.

briefdescription.para: Get the first text color from the configured sequence.

simplesect.para: The first sequence color, or the inherited color if no sequence is configured.

parameterdescription.para: The single base color to use for the rendered text.

briefdescription.para: Get the optional font. If this is empty, regular text rendering is used.

parameterdescription.para: The font to use, or an empty pointer for regular terminal text rendering.
The animation mode to apply while rendering the text.

detaileddescription.para: For the Terminal::printParagraph calls, vertical alignment is ignored. For the drawText(Text) calls, the vertical alignment is used to align the text in the given rectangle.

briefdescription.para: Set the alignment of the paragraph.
The line indent for all lines.

detaileddescription.para: Only valid if the alignment is set to Alignment::Left. This indent can be overridden by firstLineIndent and wrappedLineIndent.

briefdescription.para: Set the line indent for all lines.

detaileddescription.para: This is the indent for the first line of the paragraph. Only valid if the alignment is set to Alignment::Left. 1:|<firstindent>Alongtextthatisbroken| 2:|intomultiplelines.|

parameterdescription.para: The new indent value. >=0 or cUseLineIndent to use lineIndent

briefdescription.para: Get the indent for wrapped lines.

detaileddescription.para: This is the indent for all lines that are wrapped at the terminal width. Only valid if the alignment is set to Alignment::Left. 1:|<firstindent>Alongtextthatisbroken| 2:|intomultiplelines.|

briefdescription.para: Set the indent for wrapped lines.

parameterdescription.para: The new indent value. >=0 or cUseLineIndent to use lineIndent

briefdescription.para: Get the margins around the paragraph.
Set the margins around the paragraph.

parameterdescription.para: The margins around the paragraph area.

detaileddescription.para: The background mode determines how the background of the paragraph is handled when lines are wrapped. It also controls how the background is extended for the last line in the paragraph.

With Text / drawText(...), cells outside the wrapped text keep the existing buffer background unless the selected mode fills them from the wrapped text. With CursorWriter::printParagraph(), indentation and padding are written as spaces, so cells not covered by wrapped-text background use the writer's current background.
When using CursorWriter::printParagraph(), configure the writer background before printing if indentation or trailing padding should visually match a surrounding panel.

briefdescription.para: Get the line break end mark.

detaileddescription.para:
The line break end mark is appended to wrapped physical lines. The mark is aligned to the right edge of the available paragraph area. If the mark contains color information, it will override the background color. 1:|Alongtextthatisbroken<endmark>| 2:|intomultiplelines.|
The current line break end mark. Empty for no line break end mark.

briefdescription.para: Set the line break end mark.

parameterdescription.para: The new line break end mark. Must not exceed two characters.

briefdescription.para: Get the line break start mark.

detaileddescription.para:
The line break start mark prepends wrapped continuation lines in left-aligned paragraphs. The mark is inserted after the continuation indentation. If the mark contains color information, it will override the background color. 1:|Alongtextthatisbroken| 2:|<startmark>intomultiplelines.|
The current line break start mark. Empty for no line break start mark.

briefdescription.para: Set the line break start mark.

parameterdescription.para: The new line break start mark. Must not exceed two characters.

detaileddescription.para: The behavior of the paragraph spacing depends on the used interface. For Terminal::printParagraph() and CursorBuffer::printParagraph(), embedded newlines are hard line breaks inside one printed paragraph, and the configured spacing is appended once after the whole call. For drawText(Text) calls, each newline starts a new paragraph and spacing is inserted between paragraphs.

briefdescription.para: Get the configured word separators as a canonicalized character string.

detaileddescription.para: Word separators split a source line into words. Consecutive separators are rendered as a single space between words. Tabs use special tab-stop handling in left-aligned paragraphs before separator handling is applied. The returned string is duplicate-free and normalized for comparison and display.

Tabs remain special tab stops in left-aligned paragraphs and act as regular word separators in other alignments when included here. The provided string is canonicalized automatically and mapped to shared defaults for common patterns.
separators
A string of Unicode characters that are used to split words.
The word break mark is used when a long word had to be split in a paragraph.

A value >0 limits the automatic wraps for one source line. Once the limit is reached, the current source line is truncated and paragraphEllipsisMark() is appended if configured. Embedded line breaks start a new source line and therefore reset the wrap counter.
The maximum number of line wraps, or zero for unlimited line wraps.

briefdescription.para: Set the maximum number of line wraps.

parameterdescription.para: The maximum number of line wraps, or zero for unlimited line wraps.

detaileddescription.para:
This string is used to indicate that a paragraph would have to be wrapped over even more lines to be displayed completely. A single character can be used, but a short text like (more…) works as well. Please note that the width of this mark further reduces the available space for the paragraph text.
The paragraph ellipsis mark or an empty string if no ellipsis mark shall be used.

We recommend using a single character or a very short text. Longer texts quickly make paragraph rendering impossible.
mark
The paragraph ellipsis mark. If empty, no ellipsis mark will be used.

briefdescription.para: Get the tab stops for the paragraph.

detaileddescription.para: Only valid if the alignment is set to Alignment::Left. If a line (text up to a newline character) contains TAB characters, each tab character will pick the next tab-stop columns value from this array. If the column is larger than the current column, spacing is inserted until the cursor reaches the tab-stop column. If the tab column is smaller, or there is no further tab stop in the sequence, tabOverflowBehavior() is used to decide whether the tab becomes a single space or starts a wrapped continuation line. In centered or right-aligned paragraphs, tabs use wordSeparators instead of these tab stops. The special value cTabWrappedLineIndent can be used to use the same indent as for wrapped lines.

briefdescription.para: Get the overflow handling for non-advancing tab stops.

detaileddescription.para: This mode is used if a TAB resolves to a tab-stop column that is not larger than the current column, or if there is no further configured tab stop.

briefdescription.para: Set the overflow handling for non-advancing tab stops.

parameterdescription.para: The behavior to use for tabs that do not advance the current line.

briefdescription.para: Get the error resolution if a paragraph cannot be rendered with the given settings.

detaileddescription.para:
If the screen layout and the given parameters do not allow the paragraph to be rendered properly, this error resolution is used.
The error resolution to use when a paragraph cannot be rendered.

parameterdescription.para: The error resolution to use when a paragraph cannot be rendered.

briefdescription.para: Describes a text block to render into a Buffer.

simplesect.para: Creating and copying text instances is expensive. Please keep and reuse created instances.
